2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following guide explains how to build a Pi SDK for any programming language, and how to integrate app-to-user (A2U)\npayments into it. It walks you through how to use the various building blocks to achieve the A2U payment process.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCode snippets in this documentation are meant as high-level examples of the required steps, and will NOT run\nif they are copy-pasted into an existing project. They are written in Javascript, but the whole point of\nthis document is to explain how to build a Pi SDK in \u003cem\u003eANY language other\u003c/em\u003e than JS.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you need to integrate Pi into your Node.JS project, you're looking for the\n\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/pi-apps/pi-nodejs\"\u003ePi SDK for Node.JS\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eBuilding blocks\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-building-blocks\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Building blocks\" href=\"#building-blocks\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA2U payments involve both interacting with the Pi Blockchain and the Pi backend. The Pi blockchain is obviously the source of truth for exchanging Pi. The Pi backend is used to improve the end-user experience (e.g. provide users understandable memos on their wallet, and link backs to your app, while preserving user and developer privacy by not making this information public on the chain) and to assist developers in avoiding to make payment mistakes (e.g. double payments due to server faults)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYour Pi SDK implementation needs to both be able to sign and submit blockchain transactions using the Stellar SDK and send additional API calls to the Pi backend using standard https libraries. As a reference, here is the list of the \u003ca href=\"https://developers.stellar.org/docs/tools-and-sdks#sdk-library\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eofficial Stellar SDK\u003c/a\u003e's in various languages. You will need to pick the one that is applicable to the language you are working on, include it as dependency into your SDK, and use it as needed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eIntegration Guide\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-integration-guide\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Integration Guide\" href=\"#integration-guide\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eOverall flow\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-overall-flow\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Overall flow\" href=\"#overall-flow\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThere are certain steps you need to follow to make A2U payment. Before we take a look at the overall flow, there's one thing to keep in mind.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eimportant\u003c/strong\u003e At the moment, you can process only one A2U payment at a time, due to the nature of sequence number requirement on the Pi Blockchain. This is a safety feature of the blockchain. However, we will support batch payments, \u003cem\u003ei.e. creating an A2U payment with multiple user uids,\u003c/em\u003e to increase the throughput. This is possible through simply adding more \u003cem\u003eoperations\u003c/em\u003e into each \u003cem\u003etransaction\u003c/em\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou essentiall need to encapsulate the following API calls into convenient SDK functions:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSend API request to the Pi server to create a payment\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSimilarly to the U2A payments where app developers create a payment using the Pi SDK's \u003ccode\u003ecreatePayment\u003c/code\u003e method, you need to implement a method that creates a payment through an API endpoint \u003ccode\u003eapi.minepi.com/v2/payments\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUnlike U2A payment, which requires the Server-Side Approval, A2U payments do not require any approval from the developer\nbefore a transaction can be sumitted, since the flow starts from the app side and not from the user's side.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003col start=\"2\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLoad the account\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou need to look up the account every time before submitting a transaction, even if the Pi server gave you its address in the past. That's because the account could have been destroyed on the blockchain or the user could have changed their active wallet. By following this recommendation, developers can save the transaction fee that might have been wasted and avoid sending Pi to the wrong address. Remember that the transaction fee still gets spent even if a transaction fails.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003col start=\"3\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBuild the transaction\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou need to build a transaction with the relevant data such as the recipient's wallet address.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003col start=\"4\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSign the transaction\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAfter you build the transaction, you need to sign with the developer's keypair. Retrieving the keypair requires the secret seed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003col start=\"5\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eSubmit the transaction to the Pi blockchain\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is when the transaction is sent to the Pi blockchain. Depending on the status, you will get a corresponding response.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003col start=\"6\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eComplete the payment by sending an API request to the \u003ccode\u003e/complete\u003c/code\u003e endpoint\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe transaction will be verified by the Pi server after you submit it in the previous step. Send an API request to the \u003ccode\u003e/complete\u003c/code\u003e endpoint to check the status and complete the payment.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eExample integration:\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-example-integration\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Example integration:\" href=\"#example-integration\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 Pi Network SDK Integration Guide","","The following guide explains how to build a Pi SDK for any programming language, and how to integrate app-to-user (A2U)","payments into it. It walks you through how to use the various building blocks to achieve the A2U payment process.","","Code snippets in this documentation are meant as high-level examples of the required steps, and will NOT run","if they are copy-pasted into an existing project. They are written in Javascript, but the whole point of","this document is to explain how to build a Pi SDK in _ANY language other_ than JS.","","If you need to integrate Pi into your Node.JS project, you're looking for the","[Pi SDK for Node.JS](https://github.com/pi-apps/pi-nodejs).","","","## Building blocks","","A2U payments involve both interacting with the Pi Blockchain and the Pi backend. The Pi blockchain is obviously the source of truth for exchanging Pi. The Pi backend is used to improve the end-user experience (e.g. provide users understandable memos on their wallet, and link backs to your app, while preserving user and developer privacy by not making this information public on the chain) and to assist developers in avoiding to make payment mistakes (e.g. double payments due to server faults)","","Your Pi SDK implementation needs to both be able to sign and submit blockchain transactions using the Stellar SDK and send additional API calls to the Pi backend using standard https libraries. As a reference, here is the list of the [official Stellar SDK](https://developers.stellar.org/docs/tools-and-sdks#sdk-library)'s in various languages. You will need to pick the one that is applicable to the language you are working on, include it as dependency into your SDK, and use it as needed.","","","## Integration Guide","","### Overall flow","","There are certain steps you need to follow to make A2U payment. Before we take a look at the overall flow, there's one thing to keep in mind. ","","\u003e **important** At the moment, you can process only one A2U payment at a time, due to the nature of sequence number requirement on the Pi Blockchain. This is a safety feature of the blockchain. However, we will support batch payments, *i.e. creating an A2U payment with multiple user uids,* to increase the throughput. This is possible through simply adding more _operations_ into each _transaction_.","","You essentiall need to encapsulate the following API calls into convenient SDK functions:","","1. Send API request to the Pi server to create a payment","\u003e Similarly to the U2A payments where app developers create a payment using the Pi SDK's `createPayment` method, you need to implement a method that creates a payment through an API endpoint `api.minepi.com/v2/payments`.","- Unlike U2A payment, which requires the Server-Side Approval, A2U payments do not require any approval from the developer","before a transaction can be sumitted, since the flow starts from the app side and not from the user's side.","","2. Load the account","\u003e You need to look up the account every time before submitting a transaction, even if the Pi server gave you its address in the past. That's because the account could have been destroyed on the blockchain or the user could have changed their active wallet. By following this recommendation, developers can save the transaction fee that might have been wasted and avoid sending Pi to the wrong address. Remember that the transaction fee still gets spent even if a transaction fails.","","3. Build the transaction","\u003e You need to build a transaction with the relevant data such as the recipient's wallet address.","","4. Sign the transaction","\u003e After you build the transaction, you need to sign with the developer's keypair. Retrieving the keypair requires the secret seed.","","5. Submit the transaction to the Pi blockchain","\u003e This is when the transaction is sent to the Pi blockchain. Depending on the status, you will get a corresponding response.","","6. Complete the payment by sending an API request to the `/complete` endpoint","\u003e The transaction will be verified by the Pi server after you submit it in the previous step. Send an API request to the `/complete` endpoint to check the status and complete the payment.","","","### Example integration:","","We are going to follow each step shown in the \"Overall flow\" section above to make an A2U payment.","","It is worth noting that although `js-stellar-sdk` is available both on the frontend and backend,","since the A2U is a server-side operation, we are providing Node.js examples.","","1. Send API request to the Pi server to create a payment","","```javascript","  const axios = require('axios');","","  // API Key of your app, available in the Pi Developer Portal","  // DO NOT hardcode this, read it from an environment variable and treat it like a production secret.","  const PI_API_KEY = \"YOUR_PI_API_KEY\"","","  const axiosClient = axios.create({baseURL: 'https://api.minepi.com', timeout: 20000});","  const config = {headers: {'Authorization': `Key ${PI_API_KEY}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'}};","  ","  // This is the user UID of this payment's recipient","  const userUid = \"a1111111-aaaa-bbbb-2222-ccccccc3333d\" // this is just an example uid!","  const body =  {amount: 1, memo: \"Memo for user\", metadata: {test: \"your metadata\"}, uid: userUid}; // your payment data and uid","  ","  let paymentIdentifier;","  let recipientAddress;","","  axiosClient.post(`/v2/payments`, body, config).then(response =\u003e {","    paymentIdentifier = response.data.identifier;","    recipientAddress = response.data.recipient;","  });","  ","```","","2. Load the account","","```javascript","const StellarSdk = require('stellar-sdk');","","const myPublicKey = \"G_YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY\" // your public key, starts with G","","// an object that let you communicate with the Pi Testnet","// if you want to connect to Pi Mainnet, use 'https://api.mainnet.minepi.com' instead","const piTestnet = new StellarSdk.Server('https://api.testnet.minepi.com');","","let myAccount;","piTestnet.loadAccount(myPublicKey).then(response =\u003e myAccount = response);","","let baseFee;","piTestnet.fetchBaseFee().then(response =\u003e baseFee = response);","```","","3. Build the transaction","","```javascript","// create a payment operation which will be wrapped in a transaction","let payment = StellarSdk.Operation.payment({","  destination: recipientAddress,","  asset: StellarSdk.Asset.native(),","  amount: body.amount.toString()","});","","// 180 seconds timeout","let timebounds;","piTestnet.fetchTimebounds(180).then(response =\u003e timebounds = response);","","let transaction = new StellarSdk.TransactionBuilder(myAccount, {","  fee: baseFee,","  networkPassphrase: \"Pi Testnet\", // use \"Pi Network\" for mainnet transaction","  timebounds: timebounds","})",".addOperation(payment)","// IMPORTANT! DO NOT forget to include the payment id as memo",".addMemo(StellarSdk.Memo.text(paymentIdentifier));","transaction = transaction.build();","```","","4. Sign the transaction","","```javascript","// See the \"Obtain your wallet's private key\" section above to get this.","// And DON'T HARDCODE IT, treat it like a production secret.","const mySecretSeed = \"S_YOUR_SECRET_SEED\"; // NEVER expose your secret seed to public, starts with S","const myKeypair = StellarSdk.Keypair.fromSecret(mySecretSeed);","transaction.sign(myKeypair);","```","","5. Submit the transaction to the Pi blockchain","","```javascript","let txid;","piTestnet.submitTransaction(transaction).then(response =\u003e txid = response.id);","```","","6. Complete the payment by sending API request to `/complete` endpoint","","```javascript","// check if the response status is 200 ","let completeResponse;","axiosClient.post(`/v2/payments/${paymentIdentifier}/complete`, {txid}, config).then(response =\u003e completeResponse = BlobContent-module__markdownBlob__T8jpG" data-hpc="true" containertiming="hpc"><article class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Pi Network SDK Integration Guide</h1><a id="user-content-pi-network-sdk-integration-guide" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Pi Network SDK Integration Guide" href="#pi-network-sdk-integration-guide"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The following guide explains how to build a Pi SDK for any programming language, and how to integrate app-to-user (A2U)
payments into it. It walks you through how to use the various building blocks to achieve the A2U payment process.</p>
<p dir="auto">Code snippets in this documentation are meant as high-level examples of the required steps, and will NOT run
if they are copy-pasted into an existing project. They are written in Javascript, but the whole point of
this document is to explain how to build a Pi SDK in <em>ANY language other</em> than JS.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you need to integrate Pi into your Node.JS project, you're looking for the
<a href="https://github.com/pi-apps/pi-nodejs">Pi SDK for Node.JS</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Building blocks</h2><a id="user-content-building-blocks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building blocks" href="#building-blocks"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A2U payments involve both interacting with the Pi Blockchain and the Pi backend. The Pi blockchain is obviously the source of truth for exchanging Pi. The Pi backend is used to improve the end-user experience (e.g. provide users understandable memos on their wallet, and link backs to your app, while preserving user and developer privacy by not making this information public on the chain) and to assist developers in avoiding to make payment mistakes (e.g. double payments due to server faults)</p>
<p dir="auto">Your Pi SDK implementation needs to both be able to sign and submit blockchain transactions using the Stellar SDK and send additional API calls to the Pi backend using standard https libraries. As a reference, here is the list of the <a href="https://developers.stellar.org/docs/tools-and-sdks#sdk-library" rel="nofollow">official Stellar SDK</a>'s in various languages. You will need to pick the one that is applicable to the language you are working on, include it as dependency into your SDK, and use it as needed.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Integration Guide</h2><a id="user-content-integration-guide" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Integration Guide" href="#integration-guide"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Overall flow</h3><a id="user-content-overall-flow" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Overall flow" href="#overall-flow"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">There are certain steps you need to follow to make A2U payment. Before we take a look at the overall flow, there's one thing to keep in mind.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>important</strong> At the moment, you can process only one A2U payment at a time, due to the nature of sequence number requirement on the Pi Blockchain. This is a safety feature of the blockchain. However, we will support batch payments, <em>i.e. creating an A2U payment with multiple user uids,</em> to increase the throughput. This is possible through simply adding more <em>operations</em> into each <em>transaction</em>.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">You essentiall need to encapsulate the following API calls into convenient SDK functions:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Send API request to the Pi server to create a payment</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Similarly to the U2A payments where app developers create a payment using the Pi SDK's <code>createPayment</code> method, you need to implement a method that creates a payment through an API endpoint <code>api.minepi.com/v2/payments</code>.</p>
</blockquote>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Unlike U2A payment, which requires the Server-Side Approval, A2U payments do not require any approval from the developer
before a transaction can be sumitted, since the flow starts from the app side and not from the user's side.</li>
</ul>
<ol start="2" dir="auto">
<li>Load the account</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">You need to look up the account every time before submitting a transaction, even if the Pi server gave you its address in the past. That's because the account could have been destroyed on the blockchain or the user could have changed their active wallet. By following this recommendation, developers can save the transaction fee that might have been wasted and avoid sending Pi to the wrong address. Remember that the transaction fee still gets spent even if a transaction fails.</p>
</blockquote>
<ol start="3" dir="auto">
<li>Build the transaction</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">You need to build a transaction with the relevant data such as the recipient's wallet address.</p>
</blockquote>
<ol start="4" dir="auto">
<li>Sign the transaction</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">After you build the transaction, you need to sign with the developer's keypair. Retrieving the keypair requires the secret seed.</p>
</blockquote>
<ol start="5" dir="auto">
<li>Submit the transaction to the Pi blockchain</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">This is when the transaction is sent to the Pi blockchain. Depending on the status, you will get a corresponding response.</p>
</blockquote>
<ol start="6" dir="auto">
<li>Complete the payment by sending an API request to the <code>/complete</code> endpoint</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">The transaction will be verified by the Pi server after you submit it in the previous step. Send an API request to the <code>/complete</code> endpoint to check the status and complete the payment.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Example integration:</h3><a id="user-content-example-integration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Example integration:" href="#example-integration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">We are going to follow each step shown in the "Overall flow" section above to make an A2U payment.</p>
<p dir="auto">It is worth noting that although <code>js-stellar-sdk</code> is available both on the frontend and backend,
since the A2U is a server-side operation, we are providing Node.js examples.</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Send API request to the Pi server to create a payment</li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">axios</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">require</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">'axios'</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

  <span class="pl-c">// API Key of your app, available in the Pi Developer Portal</span>
  <span class="pl-c">// DO NOT hardcode this, read it from an environment variable and treat it like a production secret.</span>
  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-c1">PI_API_KEY</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"YOUR_PI_API_KEY"</span>

  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">axiosClient</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">axios</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">create</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-c1">baseURL</span>: <span class="pl-s">'https://api.minepi.com'</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">timeout</span>: <span class="pl-c1">20000</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">config</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-c1">headers</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s">'Authorization'</span>: <span class="pl-s">`Key <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">${</span><span class="pl-c1">PI_API_KEY</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>`</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s">'Content-Type'</span>: <span class="pl-s">'application/json'</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  
  <span class="pl-c">// This is the user UID of this payment's recipient</span>
  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">userUid</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"a1111111-aaaa-bbbb-2222-ccccccc3333d"</span> <span class="pl-c">// this is just an example uid!</span>
  <span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">body</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span>  <span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-c1">amount</span>: <span class="pl-c1">1</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">memo</span>: <span class="pl-s">"Memo for user"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">metadata</span>: <span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-c1">test</span>: <span class="pl-s">"your metadata"</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">uid</span>: <span class="pl-s1">userUid</span><span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span> <span class="pl-c">// your payment data and uid</span>
  
  <span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">paymentIdentifier</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  <span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">recipientAddress</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

  <span class="pl-s1">axiosClient</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">post</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">`/v2/payments`</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">body</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-s1">config</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
    <span class="pl-s1">paymentIdentifier</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">identifier</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
    <span class="pl-s1">recipientAddress</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">data</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">recipient</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
  <span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="  const axios = require('axios');

  // API Key of your app, available in the Pi Developer Portal
  // DO NOT hardcode this, read it from an environment variable and treat it like a production secret.
  const PI_API_KEY = "YOUR_PI_API_KEY"

  const axiosClient = axios.create({baseURL: 'https://api.minepi.com', timeout: 20000});
  const config = {headers: {'Authorization': `Key ${PI_API_KEY}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'}};
  
  // This is the user UID of this payment's recipient
  const userUid = "a1111111-aaaa-bbbb-2222-ccccccc3333d" // this is just an example uid!
  const body =  {amount: 1, memo: "Memo for user", metadata: {test: "your metadata"}, uid: userUid}; // your payment data and uid
  
  let paymentIdentifier;
  let recipientAddress;

  axiosClient.post(`/v2/payments`, body, config).then(response => {
    paymentIdentifier = response.data.identifier;
    recipientAddress = response.data.recipient;
  });
  " tabindex="0" role="button">

<ol start="2" dir="auto">
<li>Load the account</li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">require</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">'stellar-sdk'</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">myPublicKey</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"G_YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY"</span> <span class="pl-c">// your public key, starts with G</span>

<span class="pl-c">// an object that let you communicate with the Pi Testnet</span>
<span class="pl-c">// if you want to connect to Pi Mainnet, use 'https://api.mainnet.minepi.com' instead</span>
<span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">piTestnet</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-k">new</span> <span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">Server</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s">'https://api.testnet.minepi.com'</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">myAccount</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-s1">piTestnet</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">loadAccount</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">myPublicKey</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-s1">myAccount</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">baseFee</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-s1">piTestnet</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fetchBaseFee</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-s1">baseFee</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="const StellarSdk = require('stellar-sdk');

const myPublicKey = "G_YOUR_PUBLIC_KEY" // your public key, starts with G

// an object that let you communicate with the Pi Testnet
// if you want to connect to Pi Mainnet, use 'https://api.mainnet.minepi.com' instead
const piTestnet = new StellarSdk.Server('https://api.testnet.minepi.com');

let myAccount;
piTestnet.loadAccount(myPublicKey).then(response => myAccount = response);

let baseFee;
piTestnet.fetchBaseFee().then(response => baseFee = response);" tabindex="0" role="button">

<ol start="3" dir="auto">
<li>Build the transaction</li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c">// create a payment operation which will be wrapped in a transaction</span>
<span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">payment</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">Operation</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">payment</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">destination</span>: <span class="pl-s1">recipientAddress</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">asset</span>: <span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">Asset</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">native</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">amount</span>: <span class="pl-s1">body</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">amount</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">toString</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-c">// 180 seconds timeout</span>
<span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">timebounds</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-s1">piTestnet</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fetchTimebounds</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-c1">180</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-s1">timebounds</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>

<span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">transaction</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-k">new</span> <span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">TransactionBuilder</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">myAccount</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-kos">{</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">fee</span>: <span class="pl-s1">baseFee</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">networkPassphrase</span>: <span class="pl-s">"Pi Testnet"</span><span class="pl-kos">,</span> <span class="pl-c">// use "Pi Network" for mainnet transaction</span>
  <span class="pl-c1">timebounds</span>: <span class="pl-s1">timebounds</span>
<span class="pl-kos">}</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">addOperation</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">payment</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span>
<span class="pl-c">// IMPORTANT! DO NOT forget to include the payment id as memo</span>
<span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">addMemo</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">Memo</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">text</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">paymentIdentifier</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-s1">transaction</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">transaction</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">build</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="// create a payment operation which will be wrapped in a transaction
let payment = StellarSdk.Operation.payment({
  destination: recipientAddress,
  asset: StellarSdk.Asset.native(),
  amount: body.amount.toString()
});

// 180 seconds timeout
let timebounds;
piTestnet.fetchTimebounds(180).then(response => timebounds = response);

let transaction = new StellarSdk.TransactionBuilder(myAccount, {
  fee: baseFee,
  networkPassphrase: "Pi Testnet", // use "Pi Network" for mainnet transaction
  timebounds: timebounds
})
.addOperation(payment)
// IMPORTANT! DO NOT forget to include the payment id as memo
.addMemo(StellarSdk.Memo.text(paymentIdentifier));
transaction = transaction.build();" tabindex="0" role="button">

<ol start="4" dir="auto">
<li>Sign the transaction</li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c">// See the "Obtain your wallet's private key" section above to get this.</span>
<span class="pl-c">// And DON'T HARDCODE IT, treat it like a production secret.</span>
<span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">mySecretSeed</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"S_YOUR_SECRET_SEED"</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span> <span class="pl-c">// NEVER expose your secret seed to public, starts with S</span>
<span class="pl-k">const</span> <span class="pl-s1">myKeypair</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-v">StellarSdk</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">Keypair</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">fromSecret</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">mySecretSeed</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-s1">transaction</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">sign</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">myKeypair</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="// See the "Obtain your wallet's private key" section above to get this.
// And DON'T HARDCODE IT, treat it like a production secret.
const mySecretSeed = "S_YOUR_SECRET_SEED"; // NEVER expose your secret seed to public, starts with S
const myKeypair = StellarSdk.Keypair.fromSecret(mySecretSeed);
transaction.sign(myKeypair);" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">let</span> <span class="pl-s1">txid</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span>
<span class="pl-s1">piTestnet</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">submitTransaction</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">transaction</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-en">then</span><span class="pl-kos">(</span><span class="pl-s1">response</span> <span class="pl-c1">=></span> <span class="pl-s1">txid</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">response</span><span class="pl-kos">.</span><span class="pl-c1">id</span><span class="pl-kos">)</span><span class="pl-kos">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<li>Complete the payment by sending API request to <code>/complete</code> endpoint</li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c">// check if the response status is 200 </span>
